data.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) of the docs-only plan "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern".

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this chunk passes):
- Mechanism correctness: for a docs deliverable the load-bearing risk is honesty/completeness; AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)") forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern flagged in 5fd2's review. Sound.
- Fitness-for-purpose: the deliverable solves the named problem ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented"), not a proxy; ACs map 1:1 to the topics needing honest documentation, including the Pass-4 coach (move registry + delivery + advisory-only), consistent with 75a9's design.
- Approach selection: the only structurally-different choice for docs-only work is duplicate-the-workflow-engine-content vs cross-reference; the plan chose cross-reference ("the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"), which avoids a divergence/duplication anti-pattern and respects 6f2d WS-DOC as single source of truth. Defensible — generated alternative discarded, not written into the plan.
- Positive rationale: present ("must be honestly documented" for a broad client base).

FINDING:
- [ ] Reconcile the "global enable/disable kill-switch" the plan tasks the docs to document against the canonical source of truth, which says NO kill-switch exists. The plan's What and AC#1 both require documenting "the global enable/disable kill-switch," but docs/plan-review-gate.md:180 (the canonical doc this plan cross-references) states the opposite: "Turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" — the only config key is the opt-in verify.require_plan_review_for_claim. As scoped, the docs would describe a feature that does not exist, contradicting the very anchor they cross-reference — a fitness-for-purpose defect for an "honestly documented" deliverable. Either re-word the config-surface item to document the opt-in toggle ("disable = turn off the opt-in; rollback is ordinary, no separate kill-switch") so it matches docs/plan-review-gate.md:180, or, if a true global kill-switch is genuinely intended, raise that as a behavior change against the build tickets rather than silently introducing it through docs.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "What" (config surface: "the global enable/disable kill-switch") + AC#1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)"); contradicted by docs/plan-review-gate.md:180
  - evidence: plan: "the global enable/disable kill-switch"; canonical doc docs/plan-review-gate.md:180: "**Turning it off is the rollback** — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed."
  - suggested_fix: Re-word the config-surface bullet and AC#1 to document the opt-in toggle (verify.require_plan_review_for_claim) and frame "disable" as turning that opt-in off (the rollback), removing the "kill-switch" framing so the docs agree with docs/plan-review-gate.md:180.
